eval.scope_note: Final re-run against repository evidence through Mission 020. Sponsor approved PASS_NAMED_POC for the named synthetic-first internal/private POC. It does not approve real personal data, live Garmin integration, live Telegram bot behavior, public deployment, production infrastructure, required gates, runtime authority, or Factory V2 removal.
eval.poc_project: V3_POC_App_Creation - Personal Performance OS synthetic-first local/private workbench

score_rationale.v3_only_compliance: Missions 002-020 record Factory V2 not used; later missions explicitly forbid V2 fallback and no standalone gap required V2.
score_rationale.mission_clarity: Mission envelopes consistently define objective, authority, authorized files, allowed commands, verification, halt rules, and reentry rules where needed.
score_rationale.scope_discipline: Executed work maps to mission authority. Real data, credentials, live integrations, public deployment, app dependency changes, and Factory V2 fallback were avoided.
score_rationale.app_completeness: The synthetic-first local/private PPOS workbench is substantial and privately deployable, with import, workflow, timeline, evidence, recommendation, review, rollback, and Garmin-shaped synthetic bridge behavior. It remains a first POC rather than product-complete: real-data boundary, live Telegram, live ambient runtime, and production use are still unapproved.
score_rationale.verification_quality: The final re-run passed 170 tests, parsed 18 mission records, and rests on per-mission verifiers, QA scripts, audit JSON, Browser notes, and deployment smoke evidence.
score_rationale.deployment_evidence: Mission 020 completed private Tailscale Serve smoke through the advertised MagicDNS tailnet route, then reset Serve and removed local deployment artifacts.
score_rationale.evidence_replay: Mission records, closeouts, checkpoints, mission state, interrupts, audit JSON, QA notes, deployment evidence, and eval artifacts reconstruct intent, authority, work, verification, failures, recovery, and rollback.
score_rationale.halt_behavior: Mission 015 proves failed-verification halt; Mission 016 proves separately authorized recovery; Mission 017 proves stale-reentry halt; Mission 018 proves fallback/no-go without V2 fallback.
score_rationale.adaptive_mission_control: Mission 012 proves genuine interrupt lifecycle, fresh-session resume, checkpoint commits, and budget fields. Mission 013 proves remote phone interrupts and another fresh resume. Later negative/deployment missions preserve authored state and checkpoints where needed.
score_rationale.dependency_discipline: No app packages were added. Garmin stayed research/synthetic/manual-export design only. Hermes was not used. Tailscale was approved host/private-network infrastructure and Serve config was reset.
score_rationale.operator_friction: Usable as a first baseline but still heavy: checkpoint bookkeeping, record schema history, commit hash synchronization, and manual eval review remain meaningful overhead.

decision_rationale: PASS_NAMED_POC is approved by the sponsor because the POC clears the rubric bar: 20/22 total, no zero scores, V3-only compliance score 2, appropriate app/verification/replay minimums, negative-path evidence, adaptive mission evidence, and private Tailscale deployment evidence. The decision is scoped to the named synthetic-first internal/private POC. Remaining real-data, live Telegram, ambient runtime, and production concerns are separately governed residual limitations, not implicit approvals.
limitations: Real-data boundary remains unexercised; any real Garmin/manual export, blood-test, nutrition image, or personal health data use still requires a separate mission.
Live Telegram bot behavior, scheduler/ambient runtime, notifications, OCR/vision, voice, production infrastructure, and public deployment remain unapproved.
Mission 020 raw Tailscale IP smoke returned HTTP 404; replay should use the advertised MagicDNS route.
Numeric token usage was not measured by a tool-backed token counter; checkpoint budget evidence improved but is not a full usage ledger.
Historical Mission 007-009 record schema drift remains a tooling-friction fact, even though human replay is possible.
Operator friction remains non-trivial and should be reduced before broader release.

verification_outputs: python3 -B -m unittest discover -s tests: 170 tests passed during final re-run
18 mission records parsed during final re-run
deployment_outputs: Mission 020 Serve reset and rollback evidence

residual_risks: Real-data boundary is not exercised and must not be inferred from synthetic Garmin-shaped evidence.
Live Telegram, scheduler, notification, OCR/vision, voice, and production infrastructure remain unapproved.
Operator friction remains meaningful for broader release.
Mission 014 draft and .DS_Store are pre-existing untracked items outside this eval.
recommended_next_steps: Back-port this sponsor-approved final eval result into Factory_V3 README, PROJECT_STATE.md, ROADMAP.md, and ROADMAP_TO_FULL_VISION.md.
Run a separately approved real-data boundary mission only if the sponsor wants to exercise real data next.
Make a separately named decision on candidate V3-OP-003 long-running remote-interrupt profile using Mission 012/013 evidence.
Continue mission-formation live trials and operator-friction reduction work.
